SUMMARY Incumbents provide skilled professional recreation programming for a geographic area, several specialty fields, or a major facility.  Assignment may include providing Citywide programming in several specialty fields such as special events, aquatics, special interest classes, adaptive, youth and adult sports, tennis, youth council, civic engagement initiatives, and teens.  Incumbents develop and coordinate a variety of Citywide classes, programs, and events for citizens of all ages; coordinate, implement, maintain, and evaluate programs; and may oversee facility operations.  Responsibilities include preparing and coordinating the master schedule; staffing specific programs, including the recruitment of instructors and officials; and supervising part time staff and volunteers.  Incumbents also perform administrative duties such as compiling data, maintaining program budget, preparing reports, soliciting bids, and providing quality customer service. EDUCATION and/or EXPERIENCE Bachelor's Degree in Recreation or a related field, and three years related experien

job.title: BUSINESS MANAGER- FINANCIAL
job.description: The City of Surprise is a fast-growing community where strong financial leadership is essential to turning plans into reality. As a Business Manager - Financial, you’ll play a central role in developing and managing department budgets, analyzing complex financial data, and supporting oversight of the development agreements and contracts that shape the City’s future. This role is ideal for someone with strong skills in Excel for purposes of reconciling data from multiple sources and translating complex financial information into clear deliverables. You’ll work in a collaborative, solutions-oriented environment where finance is a strategic partner. If you enjoy digging into details, improving processes, and using your technical skills to support responsible growth and high-quality public services, the City of Surprise offers the opportunity to make a visible and lasting impact. SUMMARY Incumbents are responsible for supervising staff, oversight and managing functions within their assigned areas with supervision from the Assistant Director. When assigned as the Business Manager - Financial, this includes completing and monitoring all financial transactions and Development Agreements; participating in department audits; preparing, monitoring, and analyzing Department budgets; analyzing operations of the various Department programs; managing grants, contracts and IGA’s; and preparing financial and budget impact analysis and statements regarding possible program and operational changes within the Department.  EDUCATION and/or EXPERIENCE   Bachelor’s Degree in a related field and five years of technical experience, including three years of supervisory or lead experience; or an equivalent combination of education and experience sufficient to successfully perform the essential duties of the job such as those listed above.  Additional education cannot be substituted for experience. CERTIFICATIONS, LICENSES, REGISTRATIONS   None SELECTION PROCESS Applicants

job.title: CITY AUDITOR
job.description: WE ARE SEEKING Our ideal candidate is a principled audit leader with deep expertise in municipal finance and Yellow Book (GAGAS) standards. They possess a sophisticated understanding of complex audit controls and a proven track record in both financial and operational analysis. As a strategic communicator, they bring an extensive professional reporting background necessary to drive public transparency and provide clear, actionable insights to City leadership. ABOUT THE CITY AUDITOR The City Auditor is a City Council appointed position, responsible for conducting independent appraisals and strategic evaluation as part of the city’s audit program and annual audit plan, providing a clear and unbiased line of communication to the Mayor and City Council.  The City Auditor will bring transparency, objectivity, and accountability in financial, performance, and attestation engagements covering internal controls and compliance.     To provide for the independence of the city audit program, the city auditor will be an appointed official functionally reporting to the audit committee.  The City Auditor leads a professional staff which includes a Senior Internal Auditor and Internal Auditor. City Auditor's Mission Statement:  To provide independent, objective, accurate and timely auditing services that are designed to improve operations, cultivate transparency and accountability. ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES The duties listed below are intended only as general illustrations of the various types of work that may be performed. Specific statements of duties not included do not exclude them from the position if the work is similar, related, or a logical assignment to the position.  Appoints, supervises, directs, disciplines, and terminates employees under the supervision of the city auditor Complies will all city policies and procedures of the city unless inconsistent with Municipal Code Develops a flexible, risk-based inter

job.title: Administrative Assistant
job.description: (Anticipated hiring range $21.64 - $22.73/hr) Positions assigned to this class perform a variety of administrative and clerical support duties involved in the day-to-day operations of a department. Incumbents assist in the preparation and administration of the annual budget and time and attendance processing. Work involves service to the public and office support as needed. Specific tasks vary depending on area of assignment.

job.title: Public Works Operations Manager (Transportation/Traffic)
job.description: This position will be responsible for managing the Streets Maintenance, Pavement Preservation, and administrative staff in our Public Works Department. A background in Engineering is helpful for this role as well as project management and contract management.  This job classification description is intended to be generic in nature and not an exhaustive list of all duties and responsibilities.  The specific tasks/duties may vary based on position assignment and as determined by the Town of Queen Creek. JOB CLASSIFICATION SUMMARY   Positions assigned to this class are responsible for administering assigned programs.  Incumbents are responsible and accountable for section planning and are charged with program supervision and implementation of services and/or programs. DISTINGUISHING CHARACTERISTICS This is a management level position, recognized as a supervisory and/or organizational authority within a specific discipline and demonstrates specific, broad practical knowledge in a major functional area.  Work involves directing, planning and coordinating staff and projects/programs, and budget. Assists with policy development within assigned functional area or the organization.   Typically, is a second-level supervisor.
